questions.objective_ref: Explain the difference between an evidence-based recommendation and a personal opinion
Identify the three parts of a well-formed recommendation: findings summary, evidence anchor, and recommendation statement
Translate rubric scores and test observations into specific, citable evidence statements
Write a short recommendation that a sceptical audience can follow and verify
Recognise and correct three common patterns that weaken a recommendation: vague claims, unsupported assertions, and ignored trade-offs
Acknowledge limitations of a rubric-based evaluation honestly and briefly
